rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are valid AADL features or declared helpers in the same annex.
Use only visible AADL features or declared auxiliary variables in implementation-level AGREE assignments and guarantees.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid component type or implementation scopes depending on the scope of the referenced features.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers in AGREE code that are not present in the AADL model's configured dependencies.
When generating AGREE code, ensure scalar type names match AGREE's expected types: int, real, bool.
Validate that guarantee expressions have matching assumed and actual types to prevent type mismatch errors.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in component implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections.
Assignments in AGREE should only reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Constants and equations in AGREE must reference valid identifiers or types present in the AADL model.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use only assignments and declarations.
Do not reference non-existent AADL features or subcomponents in AGREE contracts without first resolving them in the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement in component types vs implementations
Component type contracts and their use of subcomponent ports
